origin.name: Morocco
origin.investigating_authority: Parquet (public prosecutor) for sudden or suspicious deaths
origin.death_certificate_name: Acte de Deces (in Arabic and French)
origin.death_certificate_language: Arabic and French. Certified English translation required.
origin.cremation_note: Cremation available for non-Muslims at designated facilities in Casablanca.
origin.key_document: Acte de Deces with certified English translation
origin.source: FCDO Morocco travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Rabat guidance

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de Deces (in Arabic and French)

airlines.name: easyJet
airlines.routes: CMN, RAK, AGA to LGW, MAN, LTN, BRS

special_rules: Acte de Deces is in Arabic and French. Certified English translation required.
Cremation available for non-Muslims only at designated Casablanca facilities.
Public prosecutor (parquet) involvement adds time for sudden or unexplained deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Moroccan documentation must be in certified English. The coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Rabat can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Morocco to UK Repatriation: Guidance for British Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Morocco. Repatriation to the UK takes 7-14 days. Arabic/French documentation and cargo flights from Casablanca all handled. Contact us 24/7.

destinations.name: Ireland
key_documents: Acte de Deces with certified English translation

airlines.name: Royal Air Maroc
airlines.routes: CMN to DUB (via CDG or LHR)

special_rules: Irish Embassy in Rabat handles consular matters.
Certified English translation of Acte de Deces required identically to UK repatriations.
destinations.dest_reception: The Irish funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English. The Coroner for the district is notified.
destinations.dest_consular_note: Department of Foreign Affairs emergency line: +353 1 408 2000. The Irish Embassy in Rabat can register the death and advise.
destinations.seo_title: Morocco to Ireland: Funeral Repatriation Guidance
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Morocco and needs to come home to Ireland. 7-14 days. Arabic/French documentation and cargo flights handled. Contact us 24/7.
